data.title: Rare earth stocks fell after China denied rare earth exports to defense companies prior to recent escalation, questioning Trump-Xi trade truce.
data.description: Before the recent escalation, China had already denied rare earth exports to defense companies. The trade truce between Trump and Xi has raised uncertainties, leading to a fall in rare earth stocks. This decision impacts the global market as rare earth elements are crucial in various industries, including technology and defense. Investors are closely monitoring this situation, especially as rare earth prices have skyrocketed in recent years. China's dominance in the rare earth market has posed concerns for countries reliant on these minerals.

Norway plans to buy two additional submarines from Germany and a separate procurement of long-range artillery, the defence ministry said on Friday, at a much higher cost than before partly due to high demand for military equipment.

data.title: Bybit & Block Scholes report shows early recovery signs in crypto markets. Bybit is 2nd largest exchange.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on December 5, 2025, Bybit, ranked as the second-largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume globally, has published its most recent Crypto Derivatives Analytics Report in partnership with Block Scholes. The report indicates relatively positive trends in cryptocurrency markets.

data.title: Infinix partners with PUBG MOBILE for 2025 Championship; GT 30 Pro to be official device.
data.description: Infinix is affirming its dedication to mobile esports on a global scale by utilizing the GT 30 Pro to support the 2025 PUBG MOBILE GLOBAL CHAMPIONSHIP held in Bangkok. The collaboration between Infinix and PUBG MOBILE for this event was confirmed. This announcement was made on December 5, 2025, in Hong Kong.

data.title: Stock market fluctuates, futures mixed as investors await Fed's inflation data. U.S. job cuts fell 53% in Nov but remained 24% higher YoY. Yields: 10-year bond at 4.11% and 2-year bond at 3.53%. Markets predict an 87% likelihood of Fed cutting rates in December. Notable stock movements include Netflix down 0.52% and Hewlett Packard Enterprise falling 9.17%.
data.description: U.S. stock futures were unstable on Friday after a mixed performance on Thursday. The September PCE price index was anticipated post the recent government shutdown. U.S. job cuts decreased 53% month-over-month in November to 71,321, though still 24% higher than last year, as the labor market faced challenges from soft demand, tariff pressures, and rising costs. 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.11% and the two-year bond at 3.53%. Market projected an 87% chance of the Fed cutting interest rates in December. Futures showed slight changes: Dow Jones -0.03%, S&P 500 0.18%, Nasdaq 100 0.38%, Russell 2000 -0.04%. Pre-market, SPY and QQQ were higher: SPY up 0.20% to $685.79, QQQ advanced 0.39% to $625.37. Netflix fell 0.52% despite bidding on Warner Bros Discovery Inc. Hewlett Packard Enterprise plunged 9.17% after mixed fourth-quarter results and weak first-quarter guidance. For extensive details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: ETH price breaks 5-month downtrend, hints at 170% gains vs BTC, fueling speculations of record highs.
data.description: The price of Ether recently mirrored its bullish performance in 2021 against Bitcoin. This has led to speculation of reaching new long-term highs against BTC.

data.title: Cregis and KuCoin collaborate in Dubai for "Bridge the Night: Institutional Partner Soirée" at CE LA VI Dubai.
data.description: On December 5, 2025, in Singapore, Cregis, a prominent provider of enterprise digital asset solutions, organized the "Bridge the Night: Institutional Partner Soirée" event at CE LA VI Dubai. The exclusive gathering, which took place at CE LA VI Dubai, was a joint initiative with KuCoin.

data.title: GOP to unveil GOP-only health care bill next week per Speaker Mike Johnson, expected to address ACA subsidies, with 'big developments' to come early next week.
data.description: Next week, House Republican leaders led by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) are planning to introduce a GOP-only health care bill. The bill is expected to be presented on the floor before the end of the year. Johnson revealed on Thursday that discussions were ongoing with members and a reveal is set for next week. No specifics about the legislation were provided, causing uncertainty about the inclusion of the ACA's enhanced subsidies. Stay updated for more information.
German parliament backs controversial military service law

data.title: Flex LNG appoints Marius Foss as new CEO; NYSE: FLNG; replaces Interim CEO; effective immediately.
data.description: In Hamilton, Bermuda on December 5, 2025, the Board of Flex LNG Ltd (NYSE: FLNG) has announced the appointment of Mr. Marius Foss as the new Chief Executive Officer of Flex LNG Management AS. Mr. Foss, who is currently the Interim Chief Executive, will now take on the role permanently.
Japan’s Economy Minister expects the stimulus package to have minimal inflationary effect.

data.title: Consumer confidence declines, employment decreases, ADP report shows job loss in early November, supporting potential Fed rate cut.
data.description: According to the latest weekly jobs report by ADP, the economy experienced a decline in job numbers up to early November. This drop was accompanied by a decrease in consumer confidence, leading to a reduction in employment rates. These trends are likely to support the Federal Reserve's decision to consider a cut in interest rates.

data.title: Zinzino AB shifts Certified Adviser to Tapper Partners AB in Gothenburg, Sweden on Dec. 5, 2025.
data.description: Zinzino AB has revealed that they are transitioning to a new Certified Adviser, Tapper Partners AB, as per the announcement made on December 5, 2025 in Gothenburg, Sweden. The agreement between Zinzino AB and Tapper Partners AB will see the latter taking on the responsibilities associated with the role of Certified Adviser.

data.title: SoFi prices public offering of 54.5M shares at $27.50 each, raising $1.5B; underwriters have option for 8.2M more shares.
data.description: SoFi Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: SOFI) saw a decrease in stock value as it announced the pricing of its public offering of 54.5 million shares at $27.50 per share. This move is projected to generate roughly $1.5 billion in total proceeds before deductions for underwriting discounts, commissions, and other expenses. Additionally, SoFi authorized underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 8.2 million extra shares at the same offering price, excluding discounts and commissions. This development is part of the company's strategy to further its financial standing.

data.title: AlphaTON plans to raise $420.69M for TON, AI expansion post baby-shelf exit, despite stock decline as a nano-cap.
data.description: AlphaTON, a company previously restricted within small market boundaries, is now launching a fundraising initiative targetting an ambitious $420.69 million despite facing a significant stock drop as a nano-cap entity.
